instruction: I just made some changes to my personal site and checked out master, but now I can't find those changes. Please help me find them and merge them into master.
finalMessage:
Recovered commit `74e4785` (“Move to Stanford”) and merged it into `master` via merge commit `6d86ddb`.

- Preserved as branch `recovered-move-to-stanford`
- Resolved the conflict using the Stanford content
- Confirmed recovered files match and working tree is clean
- Jekyll build unavailable because Bundler is not installed

trajectory.summary:
git status --short --branch && printf '
--- branches ---
' && git branch -avv && printf '
--- recent reflog ---
' && git reflog --date=iso -20 && printf '
--- recent graph ---
' && git log --all --decorate --onelin

printf '%s\n' '--- lost commit ---' && git show --stat --oneline --decorate 74e4785 && git show --format=fuller --no-ext-diff --find-renames 74e4785 && printf '%s\n' '--- master-only commit ---' && git show --stat --onel
git show --format=fuller --no-ext-diff d7d3e4b -- _includes/about.md && printf '\n--- current files ---\n' && sed -n '1,8p' _includes/about.md && grep -n 'PhD\|Postdoc' _layouts/default.html

printf '%s\n' '--- final status ---' && git status --short --branch && printf '%s\n' '--- recovered changes present on master ---' && git merge-base --is-ancestor 74e4785 master && git diff --exit-code 74e4785 -- _includ
